hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For 'config_abstract_factory_can_create', the output must be strictly the string 'can_create=yes' for success and the string 'can_create=no' for failure.
The specific example options string to replace is the literal JSON: {"foo":"bar"} used to illustrate distinct object creation.
For 'config_abstract_factory_error', when the config shape is missing, the output must include the literal string 'missing_config_service' as a key-value pair in the detail section.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same distinctness guarantee as provided by the build_with_options module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This references the behavior where repeated builds with identical options must yield a new, non-shared object instance, similar to how build_with_options handles option distinctness.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same operation token mapping as defined in the Action/Operation Mapping table
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This requires grepping the codebase for the internal switch logic under 'action_map' or 'operation_mapping' to see how the external operation tokens (e.g., 'service_retrieval') are handled internally.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_service_retrieval.json.description: Resolves configured entries from direct instances, factories, closures, and abstract factories, and reports whether the same entry is cached between repeated lookups.

feature1_2_sharing_rules.json.description: Applies default and per-entry sharing rules when the same configured entry is requested more than once.

feature1_3_build_with_options.json.description: Creates a fresh object with caller-supplied options using the explicit build path instead of retrieving a cached shared entry.

feature1_4_alias_resolution.json.description: Follows alias chains when checking and retrieving entries, returning the same shared target object through every alias.

feature1_5_lifecycle_hooks.json.description: Runs delegators around object creation and initializers after creation so the resolved object contains all externally visible contributions.

feature1_6_configuration_and_mutation.js.description: Adds and changes registrations after construction through configuration and mutation APIs, including factories, aliases, delegators, initializers, and sharing rules.

feature1_7_override_protection.json.description: Rejects mutation of an already configured entry unless override permission has been explicitly enabled.

feature1_8_cyclic_alias_detection.json.description: Detects cycles in alias definitions during configuration and reports a normalized alias-cycle error instead of looping.

feature2_1_configured_dependency_factory.description: Uses an explicit dependency map to decide whether an object can be created and to assemble objects with zero, simple, or nested dependencies.

feature2_2_configured_dependency_errors..description: Reports normalized errors when the explicit dependency map is missing or has a shape that cannot describe dependencies.

feature3_1_reflection_factory_capability.description: Determines whether automatic constructor inspection can create a requested entry based on whether it is a constructible class.

feature3_2_reflection_factory_creation.j.description: Creates objects by inspecting constructor requirements, injecting container services, config maps, well-known service aliases, and default values where appropriate.

feature3_3_reflection_factory_errors.jso.description: Reports normalized dependency-resolution errors when automatic constructor inspection encounters required parameters that cannot be supplied.

feature4_1_plugin_manager_validation_and.description: A constrained child container retrieves valid plugin objects, caches normal lookups, bypasses the cache when options are supplied, and rejects invalid plugin instances.

feature5_1_container_decorator.json.description: Wraps a standards-compatible container so existence checks, retrieval, and access to the wrapped container are forwarded transparently.

_constraints.output_rule: Output must be plain text lines, not JSON.
exact_values: delimiters: only newline, no extra whitespace at line ends

_constraints.output_rule: Output describes partial graph resolution (registration, result type) and (only for 'factory' reg) whether retrieved objects are shared via cache.
If 'registration' is not 'factory' (or not explicitly provided/ignored per implied behavior), output must state `has_service=no` and omit resolution details.

_constraints.feature: Feature 1.1 Service Retrieval - Factory Type Specifics
_constraints.output_rule: Output must include 'resolved=<Class>.py{ClassName}' and 'same_on_second_get=yes' to confirm caching.
Output must indicate if the alias exists and if retrieving via alias returns the same object as the target registration.
If 'allow_override' is missing or false, input action must return error `modification_not_allowed`. If true, mutation must succeed.
Output must include the specific options provided by the caller and confirm distinct object creation for repeated builds.
Output must list properties contributed by creation wrappers (delegators) and post-creation initializers, and confirm sharing.
exact_values: example_output: resolved=generic_object, option=OPTIONED, foo=bar, bar=baz, same_on_second_get=yes

_constraints.output_rule: If query returns a valid object, output resolution info. If invalid instance is present in hidden fallback scenarios (implied by 'rejected'), return error.
Should behave like 'can_create' if dependency map not met, otherwise resolve nested dependencies successfully.
exact_values: pattern: [can_create successes, can_create failures, resolution successes with nesting]

_constraints.output_rule: Output must provide normalized error and detail for missing/malformed shapes (missing map, non-string, etc.).
Must confirm consistency of the returned container and visibility of new registrations.
exact_values: example_output: has_date_before=yes, has_generic_before=no, returned_same_container=yes, has_date_after=yes, has_generic_after=yes

_constraints.output_rule: Must return 'can_create=no' for 'non_class', must return 'can_create=yes' for empty constructors or well-known types by convention.
Must normalize errors for missing typed services or unsupported types (e.g., sending a string or bytes as a typed service).
Must indicate if id is known and if wrapped container interface compatibility holds.
exact_values: services: Accessor injections (objects containing container instances)

_constraints.output_rule: Must reject cycles (e.g., 'a': 'a') or chains with no valid output.
Output must contain NO duplicates for single retrieval requests.
Mapping of full operation tokens to internal switch logic.
exact_values: full_ops: service_retrieval, sharing_rules, build_with_options, alias_resolution, delegator_initializer, configure_container, override_protection, cyclic_alias, config_abstract_factory_can_create, config_abstract_factory_create, config_abstract_factory_error, reflection_factory_can_create, reflection_factory_create, reflection_factory_error, plugin_manager, psr_decorator
